title: Why The Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte (BMV:OMA B) Narrative Is Shifting On Valuation And Risk
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte has seen its assessed fair value move from MX$262.46 to MX$259.46, a small cut that still matters if you are tracking valuation closely. This shift comes as research houses pull in different directions, with some lifting price targets by MX$8 and others cutting by as much as MX$35. These differing moves reflect mixed views on execution risk and growth durability. In the sections that follow, you will see how these target moves fit together and what to watch as the story continues to evolve.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Bradesco BBI upgraded Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte to Outperform from Neutral in early January, signaling greater confidence in how the company can execute on its current plan at the price level implied by its $120 target. The January upgrade suggests Bradesco BBI sees enough support for the long term growth story to justify a more constructive stance, even after accounting for operational and regulatory risks that are common in the sector.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Barclays has adjusted its view more cautiously, first lifting the OMA target by MX$8 in February, then cutting it by MX$35 in March. This highlights rising concern about execution risk and the strength of the growth runway at prior valuation levels. The recent Barclays reduction in the target price indicates a tighter margin for error, with less room for disappointments in traffic, capital spending or regulatory outcomes before the current valuation could come into question.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!BMV:OMA B 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 2 risks for Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte reported February 2026 operating results with 2,016,352 total passengers, compared with 1,913,133 a year earlier, and year-to-date passengers of 4,326,136 versus 4,091,640 a year earlier. For January 2026, the company reported 2,309,784 total passengers, compared with 2,178,507 in January 2025. Passenger traffic for 4Q25 was 6.0% higher than 4Q24 at 7.5 million passengers, and full-year 2025 passenger traffic was 8.5% higher at 28.8 million passengers. In December 2025, total passengers were 2,654,680 compared with 2,484,220 a year earlier, with year-to-date passengers of 28,750,694 versus 26,510,498 a year earlier.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de

Fair Value moved from MX$262.46 to MX$259.46, a reduction of about 1.1%. Revenue Growth moved from 11.01% to 11.39%. Net Profit Margin moved from 39.00% to 38.91%. Future P/E moved from 17.73x to 17.39x. Discount Rate moved from 14.23% to 14.24%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company’s business story to a financial forecast and a fair value view, so you can see how assumptions, risks and growth drivers fit together. They update over time as new data and research come through.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de to stay up to date on:

How investment in Monterrey Airport capacity and upgrades ties into long term expectations for passenger throughput and margins. The role of rising passenger volumes and higher commercial revenue per passenger in supporting earnings and cash flow assumptions. Key risks around heavy exposure to Monterrey, reliance on VivaAerobus and Volaris, regulatory tariff decisions and leadership changes that could affect execution.
